destinations.evidence: ZK Phase 1 pricing is constant for executor fee and uses a small 64-byte output for inclusion accounting.

equivalence_argument: The port does not derive ZK Phase 1 executor fee from payload/output content; it uses the ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI constant and normal validator fee helpers.
notes: This is the same fee path as the explicit-fee ZK Phase 1 mapping.
rationale: The merge introduces ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI, adds calculate_zk_fees_phase1(), and routes ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE fee calculation through it before DKMS or HTTP/LLM branches. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Phase 1 fee calculation for the ZK two-phase precompile must use ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI as the executor fee, plus normal commitment fee and minimal inclusion fee for the small commitment-hash output.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call calculate_async_fees for ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE with a representative input length and assert executor_fee equals ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI, commitment_fee equals async_commitment_fee(input_len), and the inclusion-fee input size corresponds to a small commitment-hash output.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call calculate_async_fees for ZK_TWO_PHASE_PRECOMPILE with a representative input length and assert executor_fee equals ZK_PHASE1_SETTLEMENT_FEE_WEI, commitment_fee equals async_commitment_fee(input_len), and the inclusion-fee input size corresponds to a small commitment-hash output.
title: ZK Phase 1 uses constant pricing
